title: euchiton litticola
intro: Euchiton litticola, also known as the Māori broom or broom daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and eastern Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Euchiton litticola include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions or tinctures, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, or taken internally to support immune function and digestive health.
